images.caption: Figure 2:Overview of the CLVR framework. The pipeline consists of three main components: (1) Flowchart of SFT and Proxy Prompt Reinforcement Learning; (2) Pipeline of the CLVR inference framework; (3) Schematic of theΔ\Delta-Space weight merge algorithm. Finally, the merged model from (3) is integrated into the inference framework to achieve efficient CLVR inference.
Figure 3:Architecture of the CLVR data synthesis pipeline, featuring a Perceive-Reason-Act workflow. The system is controlled by Gemini 2.5 Pro[14].
Figure 4:Visual comparison of generation results (CLVR) with other methods. Key control signals in the prompts are highlighted in bold.

Figure 5:Quantitative results of the semantic complexity probe.CtaskC_{\text{task}}stratifies prompt difficulty (entities, relations, and hard constraints);IeffI_{\text{eff}}is an entropy effective-rank spectral proxy for backbone capacity[35]. The left plot shows that CLVR achieves a higher Area Under the Pass-Complexity Curve (AUC Pass) compared to single-step models of similar capacity. The right plot illustrates that while single-step models’ performance drops sharply as task complexity increases, CLVR maintains a high pass rate across all tiers.

header: Appendix ATechnical appendices and supplementary material

images.caption: Figure 6:A step-by-step CLVR inference case. The trajectory begins with concept initialization (Step 1), followed by environment embedding (Step 2), lighting and atmosphere refinement (Step 3), and final typographic integration (Step 4), resulting in a cohesive image that fulfills all complex prompt requirements.

Figure 7:An example of training data generated by data synthesis pipeline. The system first generates a base image, then identifies a missing "AM" logo in the background and adds it. Finally, it recognizes that the rear details are missing and changes the perspective to a rear three-quarter view to include the taillight and license plate, demonstrating effective self-correction.
